prompt: Create a photorealistic tabletop product shot of a handmade layered craft-paper shadow box diorama inside a deep square black frame, showing a fantasy alchemist girl from Toxic Party standing in the center of an old laboratory. The character has long flowing white hair, a partially blurred or softly obscured face, a pale green long coat with split tails, darker green leggings, brown leather boots, crisscrossing chest straps, belt pouches, and exactly 7 small potion bottles strapped around her waist. Pose her dynamically with one arm raised in a greeting or spell-casting gesture and the other arm extended sideways. Build the entire interior as layered cut paper and cardboard with visible stacked depth: arched shelves, gothic window, wooden beams, stone-tile floor, and warm lantern light. Include exactly 24 laboratory vessels and bottles in the diorama: 7 on the left upper shelves, 6 on the left lower shelves and table, 4 on the rear middle shelves, 3 on the right shelves, 2 large floor bottles, and 2 bottles attached to the foreground furniture. Add exactly 5 major foreground props inside the box: an open sketchbook with alchemy diagrams, a low worktable, a small crate, a furnace or brazier with orange flame, and a large round-bottom flask. On the right wall, add a parchment sign reading Laboratoire de Saint-Germain Paris, 1768. Surround the framed diorama with a real craft workspace: green cutting mat, scattered paper scraps in tan, brown, sage and white, a metal ruler, precision craft knife, tweezers, a bottle of craft glue labeled CRAFT GLUE, a jar of brushes and pencils, and a cardboard box. Use shallow depth of field, warm studio lighting, realistic paper fibers, subtle shadows between every cut-paper layer, and a cozy handmade shadow-box aesthetic.
